name: Mona Lisa Moon Ship
description: The "Mona Lisa Moon Ship" theory, originating within UFO conspiracy circles in America, posits that a crashed alien spacecraft, containing the mummified body of a female alien nicknamed "Mona Lisa," was discovered on the far side of the moon by the Apollo 20 mission in 1976. This alleged mission, a joint US-Soviet venture, is claimed to have been a secret, unsanctioned operation beyond the public Apollo program. Proponents cite grainy video footage and alleged leaked information as evidence, claiming the footage shows the interior of the spacecraft and the alien being. Mainstream science and space agencies dismiss the theory entirely, citing the lack of credible evidence, the absence of any official record of Apollo 20, and the easily debunked nature of the purported 'evidence'. The theory is often linked to broader narratives about suppressed knowledge of extraterrestrial life and secret space programs. The primary source of the theory is often attributed to William Rutledge, a man claiming to be an Apollo 20 astronaut, whose claims are widely considered to be a hoax. The narrative's popularity rests on the allure of secret space exploration and the desire to believe in advanced extraterrestrial civilizations, despite the lack of verifiable support.
subtitle: Alleged crashed alien craft on the Moon

claims.claim: Apollo 20 was a real, secret mission.
claims.evidence: Supposed leaked video footage and testimony from William Rutledge.
claims.criticism: No official records exist, and Rutledge's claims are widely discredited as a hoax. No independent verification exists.

claims.claim: A crashed alien spacecraft was found on the far side of the moon.
claims.evidence: Grainy video footage allegedly showing the interior of the ship.
claims.criticism: The footage is of questionable origin and quality, easily reproducible with special effects. No verifiable confirmation from any space agency.

claims.claim: A mummified alien female, dubbed "Mona Lisa," was discovered inside the spacecraft.
claims.evidence: Rutledge's testimony and purported images of the alien.
claims.criticism: The images are often blurry and lack detail, and have been suggested to be fabricated or misinterpreted. No scientific analysis of the alleged remains exists.

keyFigures.name: William Rutledge
keyFigures.role: Claimed to be an Apollo 20 astronaut and source of the theory.

keyFigures.name: José Escamilla
keyFigures.role: UFO researcher who popularized Rutledge's claims.

keyFigures.name: Phil Plait
keyFigures.role: Astronomer and skeptic who has debunked the claims.

mythologyConnections: Ancient astronaut theories linking alien contact to mythology
Themes of hidden knowledge and suppressed truths

timeline.event: William Rutledge begins releasing information and videos online.
The theory gains traction within online UFO communities.
